generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically retrieve and refresh all Event and Activity data from Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 on a fixed 60-second cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 mandates a 60-second automated refresh cycle to ensure real-time situational awareness during live exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow an authorized user to manually pause the automatic 60-second data refresh cycle and shall resume the cycle upon explicit user command.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 permits manual pause to support briefing moments or display freeze requirements while ensuring the cycle resumes on demand.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement a Multi-Document Interface (MDI) layout that permanently allocates the left two-thirds of the display area to a graphical timeline view and the right one-third to a data table view; this ratio shall not be user-configurable.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 fixes the display layout proportions to ensure consistent projection-ready presentation across all exercise operations center deployments.

generated_requirements.text: Within the data table region, the system shall display sequential (order-dependent, non-time-bound) Events in the top one-quarter of the table area and temporally relevant Events ordered by proximity to current exercise time in the bottom three-quarters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 defines the segmentation of the table region to allow controllers to distinguish between sequential and time-critical Events at a glance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall color-code all displayed Events and Activities based on their variance from current exercise time, using threshold categories (e.g., on-time, at-risk, overdue) that conform to the DHS color schema standard.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 and BR-05 require DHS-compliant color-coded variance indicators to enable instant visual triage of schedule adherence.

generated_requirements.text: All visual elements of the application — including backgrounds, text, highlights, borders, and status indicators — shall conform to the Department of Homeland Security color schema specification; no custom or ad hoc color choices are permitted without formal DHS approval.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 and RC-01 mandate full DHS color schema compliance across all UI elements to meet regulatory and standards constraints.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a graphical timeline in the left two-thirds region that plots Events and Activities as nodes positioned according to their scheduled exercise time.
generated_requirements.rationale: The future state description requires a graphical timeline region to provide visual situational awareness of Event sequencing and timing.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display MSEL Summary data only for each Event and Activity node in the graphical timeline and each row in the data table; full MSEL detail records shall not be rendered within the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 restricts the data scope to MSEL Summary data to keep the display concise and projection-readable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a direct deep-link from each displayed Event or Activity back to its corresponding record in the originating source application (Sync Matrix 1.0 or EMT 1.0), enabling users to navigate to the source record for modification without leaving the display context.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 establishes that EEMDS is a display-only tool and all edits must occur in source applications, requiring seamless deep-link navigation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall simultaneously display two independent, visually distinct clocks at all times: one showing current local (wall-clock) time and one showing current exercise time.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 requires dual-clock display to eliminate confusion in exercises where exercise time diverges from wall-clock time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to define, configure, and modify time segments used to organize Events on the timeline, with these segments configurable per exercise prior to or during exercise execution.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 requires user-configurable time segments to support the varied temporal structures of different exercises.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support filtering of displayed Events and Activities by Venue, independently or in combination with other active filters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 mandates venue-based filtering to reduce cognitive load for controllers managing multi-venue exercises.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support filtering of displayed Events and Activities by Key Events designation, independently or in combination with other active filters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 mandates key event category filtering to allow controllers to isolate high-priority Events during fast-moving scenarios.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an on-demand unfiltered view of all Events in the exercise, accessible regardless of any currently active filters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-13 requires a full exercise view to ensure controllers can always access the complete Event set without clearing filters manually.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall capture a history record of every change to Event or Activity data — as reflected from source systems — including the field changed, previous value, new value, timestamp, and source application, and shall make this history report available for retrieval at any point during or after the exercise.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 mandates a complete audit history to support post-exercise after-action review and reconstruction of the exercise timeline.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and export an actual versus assumed time variance chart for Events and Activities, quantifying the difference between scheduled and actual execution times.
generated_requirements.rationale: The future state and in-scope section require actual vs. assumed time variance charts to support quantitative schedule adherence analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate every user at login and deny access to any user not pre-authorized by the system administrator; no guest or anonymous access modes shall be supported.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 and RC-02 require positive authentication and strict access denial for unauthorized users to protect sensitive exercise data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control, distinguishing at minimum between roles such as Exercise Controller, Exercise Planner, IT Administrator, and AAR Analyst, and restricting display configuration and history report access to appropriate roles.
generated_requirements.rationale: The stakeholder matrix and future state description require role-differentiated access to prevent unauthorized configuration or data access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with Sync Matrix 1.0 using its existing data interface (API, database connection, or file export) in read-only mode, without requiring any modification to Sync Matrix 1.0.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-02 and the out-of-scope section prohibit modifications to source applications, requiring read-only integration via existing interfaces.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with Exercise Management Tool 1.0 using its existing data interface (API, database connection, or file export) in read-only mode, without requiring any modification to EMT 1.0.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-02 and the out-of-scope section prohibit modifications to source applications, requiring read-only integration via existing interfaces.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall surface all Events and Activities from both Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0 in a single unified display without requiring manual data entry by any user.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 5 requires full consolidation of both source applications into a single display to eliminate fragmented data access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall visually indicate to the user when the data refresh cycle is paused, including the elapsed time since the last successful refresh.
generated_requirements.rationale: Users must be aware of data staleness during a paused refresh to make informed decisions during briefing or freeze scenarios.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display the timestamp of the most recent successful data refresh from each source application in a persistently visible location on the display.
generated_requirements.rationale: Displaying the last refresh timestamp allows controllers to assess data currency and detect integration failures during live exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall alert the user with a visible on-screen notification if a data refresh cycle fails to retrieve data from either source application within the expected 60-second window.
generated_requirements.rationale: Proactive failure notification prevents controllers from acting on stale data without awareness of the integration disruption.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable as a Windows-based client application on a standard PC within the exercise operations center, with no dependency on server-side rendering or cloud infrastructure for core display functionality.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-01 constrains the deployment model to a standalone Windows client to match the operations center environment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the IT Administrator to configure and manage the list of authorized users and their assigned roles within the application's access control settings.
generated_requirements.rationale: A-04 assumes user identity management is administered by the IT Administrator, requiring an administrative interface for access control configuration.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete each 60-second data refresh cycle — including retrieval from both source applications, processing, and full display update — within 10 seconds of the scheduled refresh trigger, such that data staleness never exceeds 70 seconds under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 1 requires data staleness never to exceed 60 seconds; a 10-second processing budget ensures the refresh completes within the cycle window.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render the complete MDI display — including the graphical timeline and data table — without perceptible lag or frame drop during or immediately following a data refresh cycle, as observed by a user on the client PC.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-02 requires that the 60-second refresh cycle introduces no perceptible degradation in display performance or usability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall respond to user filter interactions (applying, changing, or clearing a filter) and update the display within 3 seconds of the user action.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rapid filter response is critical during fast-moving exercise scenarios where controllers must quickly isolate relevant Events.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be available 99% of the time during normal business hours (0800–1800 local time, Monday through Friday) over the first six months of operation; planned maintenance windows shall be scheduled outside these hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 3 and OC-01 establish a 99% availability target during business hours to ensure the system is reliable during live exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render all Event and Activity text, color-coded status indicators, and timeline graph elements at font sizes and visual scales sufficient for 90% of viewers to read Event/Activity detail data from a distance of 30 feet on a 10×10 projection screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 8 sets a measurable readability criterion of 90% of viewers at 30 feet on a 10×10 projection screen.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render color-coded Event status indicators at a visual scale sufficient for 90% of viewers to interpret schedule variance status from a distance of 100 feet on a 10×10 projection screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 2 requires color-coded status to be interpretable at 100 feet to support shared situational awareness in large operations centers.

generated_requirements.text: 90% of new users shall be able to independently initiate an Event or Activity display within 90 minutes of first use, without formal training.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 4 sets a measurable learnability criterion to minimize the learning curve for exercise controllers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide contextual on-screen help or tooltips for all primary controls — including filter selectors, pause/resume, time segment configuration, and deep-link navigation — accessible without leaving the display view.
generated_requirements.rationale: Inline help supports the 90-minute independent-use learnability target by reducing reliance on external documentation during first use.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce positive user authentication using credentials validated against the authorized user list maintained by the IT Administrator, and shall deny access and log the attempt for any user whose credentials are not recognized or are not authorized.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 7 requires 100% enforcement of access control with zero unauthorized access incidents, necessitating robust authentication and denial logging.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all failed authentication attempts, including timestamp, username supplied, and client machine identifier, and shall make this log available to the IT Administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Logging failed access attempts supports DHS IT security compliance and enables detection of unauthorized access patterns.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with applicable DHS IT security policies governing user authentication, session management, and access control for internal operational tools, as defined in RC-02.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-02 mandates compliance with DHS IT security policies to satisfy regulatory and standards constraints applicable to the organization.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between EEMDS and the source applications (Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0) shall be protected against unauthorized interception using encryption appropriate to the DHS operational environment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Exercise data may be sensitive; encrypting data in transit protects against interception in the operations center network environment.

generated_requirements.text: The history report of Event and Activity data changes shall be tamper-evident; any post-generation modification to a history record shall be detectable by the system or an AAR Analyst.
generated_requirements.rationale: Tamper-evidence of the audit log ensures the integrity of after-action review data and supports reliable post-exercise reconstruction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a mean time to recovery (MTTR) of no more than 30 minutes following any unplanned outage during normal business hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: A bounded MTTR supports the 99% availability target by limiting the duration of any individual outage during exercise operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall gracefully handle the temporary unavailability of either source application by continuing to display the most recently retrieved data, clearly indicating the source and age of the stale data, without crashing or requiring a restart.
generated_requirements.rationale: D-01 and D-02 identify source application availability as a dependency; graceful degradation prevents total display failure during transient integration outages.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall preserve the history report data and all configuration settings (time segments, user roles, filter presets) across application restarts and unplanned outages without data loss.
generated_requirements.rationale: Persistence of configuration and audit data across restarts ensures continuity of exercise operations and integrity of the after-action record.

generated_requirements.text: The system's source code, configuration files, and data schemas shall be documented sufficiently to allow a qualified developer to diagnose and resolve a defect within one business day without involvement of the original development team.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adequate documentation reduces maintenance burden and supports rapid defect resolution to protect the 99% availability target.

generated_requirements.text: The integration layer connecting EEMDS to Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0 shall be implemented as a modular, independently configurable component so that changes to a source application's data interface can be accommodated without requiring changes to the display or business logic layers.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular integration design isolates source-system interface changes, reducing the cost and risk of future maintenance when source applications evolve.

generated_requirements.text: The DHS color schema thresholds for schedule variance color-coding shall be defined in an externally configurable settings file, allowing the color schema to be updated to reflect revised DHS standards without requiring a code change or redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Externalizing color schema configuration reduces the cost of compliance updates when DHS revises its color standards.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on any Windows 10 or later client PC meeting the minimum hardware specifications defined in the deployment guide, without requiring installation of non-standard runtime environments beyond those documented.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-01 requires deployment on a standard client PC; broad Windows version compatibility ensures the system can be deployed across the DHS operations center environment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render the MDI layout and all display elements correctly at screen resolutions and aspect ratios compatible with projection equipment capable of displaying a 10×10 screen image, without requiring manual layout adjustment by the user.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-04 requires correct rendering on projection equipment; resolution-independent layout ensures readability across the range of projection hardware used in operations centers.

generated_requirements.text: The history report shall be exportable in at least one open, human-readable format (e.g., CSV or PDF) to support use by AAR Analysts in external analysis tools without requiring access to the EEMDS application.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 6 requires a complete history report available at exercise conclusion; export capability ensures AAR Analysts can use the data in their preferred analysis tools.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the concurrent use of multiple filter combinations as named, saveable filter presets that authorized users can define, save, and recall during exercise execution.
generated_requirements.rationale: Saveable filter presets reduce the time required to switch between common filtering configurations during fast-moving exercise scenarios.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record and store all user session activity — including login, logout, filter changes, pause/resume actions, and deep-link navigations — with timestamps, as part of the audit log accessible to the IT Administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Comprehensive session activity logging supports DHS IT security compliance, access accountability, and post-exercise forensic review.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall refresh the display every 60 seconds.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The application shall match the color of the schema set forth by Department of Homeland Security
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If projected the data must be readable. On a 10x10 projection screen 90% of viewers must be able to read Event / Activity data from a viewing distance of 30
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be available during normal business hours. As long as the user has access to the client PC the system will be available 99% of the time during the first six months of operation.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If projected the data must be understandable. On a 10x10 projection screen 90% of viewers must be able to determine that Events or Activities are occuring in current time from a viewing distance of 100
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall ensure that it can only be accessed by authorized users. The product will be able to distinguish between authorized and unauthorized users in all access attempts
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be intuitive and self-explanatory. : 90% of new users shall be able to start the display of Events or Activities within 90 minutes of using the product.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall respond fast to keep up-to-date data in the display.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall have a MDI form that allows for the viewing of the graph and the data table.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display Events in a vertical table by time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display the Events in a graph by time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display Events or Activities.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The display shall have two regions: left 2/3 of the display is graphical right 1/3 of the display is a data table
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The data displayed in both the nodes within the graph and the rows in the table are MSEL Summary data
ground_truth_requirements.label: NONE

ground_truth_requirements.text: The table side of the display shall be split into 2 regions: sequential and temporal.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The top 1/4 of the table will hold events that are to occur sequentially.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The bottom 3/4 of the table will hold events that occur according to its relevance to current time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall color code events according to their variance from current time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display data from the Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 applications
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall link Events back to either the Sync Matrix 1.0 or the Exercise Managment Tool 1.0 applications for modifications.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall allow modification of the display.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall offer a display of all the Events in the exercise.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall filter data by: Venues and Key Events.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall allow a user to define the time segments
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display the local and exercise time in separate clocks
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall offer the ability to pause and resume the refresh of data.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall provide charts for the Activity or Event actual versus assumed time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall provide a history report of changes made to the Activity or Event data
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require every user to authenticate with valid credentials (username and password) before accessing any system function.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 and the security success criterion mandate that 100% of users are authenticated and authorized before any access is granted.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deny access to any individual who is not formally registered as a realtor in the organization's realtor registry.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 requires that unregistered individuals are denied access at all entry points to protect proprietary MLS and client data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authorize each authenticated user against the realtor registry on every session initiation, ensuring only currently registered realtors retain access.
generated_requirements.rationale: Continuous authorization enforcement prevents access by realtors who have been deregistered since their last login.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to search MLS property listings by specifying one or more criteria (e.g., location, price range, property type, number of bedrooms).
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS query capability is a core in-scope feature required to eliminate the field productivity gap described in the problem statement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display MLS search results including at least one clear property image per listing alongside key property details.
generated_requirements.rationale: The scope section explicitly requires results to include clear property images to support on-site client consultations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return MLS property search results within 30 seconds of query submission under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: The 30-second search result ceiling is a hard performance constraint defined in both the business objectives and technical constraints sections.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to save property search results to local device storage for later review.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 requires that realtors can save search results and that those results remain accessible in offline mode.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to create new property listings in the MLS from the mobile device, enforcing data validation rules before submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS listing management is in scope, and BR-02 requires data validation to prevent malformed data from entering MLS records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to update existing MLS property listings from the mobile device, enforcing data validation rules before submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: Updating existing listings is an in-scope capability required to keep MLS data current from the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all property listing fields against defined MLS data rules and reject any submission containing incorrect or malformed data, displaying a descriptive error message to the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 mandates data validation controls to protect MLS data integrity before any listing is submitted.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate a Comparative Market Analysis (CMA) report based on property criteria explicitly specified by the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: CMA report generation is a core in-scope feature, and BR-08 requires explicit criteria input before a report is produced.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not generate a CMA report unless the realtor has explicitly entered property criteria; the system shall prompt the user for required criteria if none are provided.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 explicitly prohibits generating a CMA report without user-supplied criteria to ensure report relevance and accuracy.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and deliver a completed CMA report to the realtor within 60 seconds of criteria entry.
generated_requirements.rationale: The 60-second CMA generation ceiling is a hard performance constraint defined in the business objectives and technical constraints sections.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a map showing the locations of properties in a realtor-specified area, restricted to properties within the MLS dataset accessible to the authenticated realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: Property mapping is in scope, and BR-09 restricts map and directions features to MLS-accessible properties only.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide turn-by-turn driving directions to a selected property from the realtor's current location, using an integrated third-party mapping service.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integrated driving directions eliminate the need for separate mapping tools, directly addressing the navigation overhead pain point.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict the provision of driving directions and property maps to properties that exist within the MLS dataset accessible to the authenticated realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 explicitly limits map and directions functionality to MLS-accessible properties to enforce data access boundaries.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall download and display the authenticated realtor's client contacts from the office system, synchronized at least once per hour.
generated_requirements.rationale: Contact management is in scope and BR-03 mandates hourly synchronization to keep field data current.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall download and display the authenticated realtor's appointments from the office system, synchronized at least once per hour.
generated_requirements.rationale: Appointment management is in scope and BR-03 mandates hourly synchronization to ensure appointment data is never more than 60 minutes stale.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically synchronize contacts and appointment data with the office system on a fixed schedule of once per hour without requiring any action from the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 requires fully automatic hourly synchronization to eliminate manual data refresh overhead for field agents.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the realtor immediately upon detecting new appointments during an automatic synchronization cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 requires that realtors are notified of new appointments discovered during synchronization without manual checking.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall initiate a call to a seller or buyer, using the contact phone number stored in the realtor's client records, for the purpose of scheduling an appointment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated appointment scheduling calls are an in-scope feature that directly addresses the scheduling inefficiency pain point.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict appointment-scheduling calls to contacts that are associated with the authenticated realtor's own client records.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 requires that communications are traceable to the realtor's account and limited to their own client contacts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the realtor immediately when a seller or buyer responds to an appointment request initiated by the system, without requiring the realtor to manually check for updates.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 and the appointment responsiveness success criterion require automated push notification upon client response to eliminate scheduling lag.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all appointment-scheduling communications, associating each communication record with the initiating realtor's account.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 requires that all system-initiated communications are traceable to the realtor's account for accountability and compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall operate in a read-only offline mode when internet connectivity is unavailable, allowing realtors to view previously downloaded search results, CMA reports, and appointments.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 and the future state description require offline read access to cached data to eliminate the current total loss of access in low-connectivity areas.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall queue write operations (e.g., new listings, listing updates) that cannot be completed while offline and automatically submit them to the appropriate backend system upon restoration of internet connectivity.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 requires that offline write operations are queued and submitted upon reconnection to prevent data loss.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect the transition from offline to online connectivity and automatically process the queued write operations without requiring realtor intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Seamless offline-to-online transition is critical to the elicitation strategy's focus on transition behaviour and data integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall save and restore 100% of user preferences (including search filters, display settings, and saved results) across sessions and following any system failure or connectivity interruption.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 and the data integrity success criterion require complete preference persistence to prevent loss of personalised agent settings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be installable by an untrained realtor on a supported smart device without the use of separately printed instructions, achieving a self-installation success rate of at least 90%.
generated_requirements.rationale: The self-service installation success criterion and operational constraints require that 90% of untrained realtors can install the app unaided.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be usable by realtors with no formal training on the product, employing terminology and workflows natural to the real estate profession.
generated_requirements.rationale: The no-training-requirement operational constraint and the usability success criterion demand a self-explanatory, domain-appropriate interface.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall conform to the native UI standards and conventions of the target smart device platform, including standard menus and navigation buttons, for both Windows CE and Palm OS.
generated_requirements.rationale: The UI standards technical constraint requires platform-native interface conventions to ensure familiarity and ease of use on target devices.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall run natively on devices operating Windows CE and Palm OS. No other mobile platforms are in scope for this release.
generated_requirements.rationale: The platform technical constraint explicitly limits the target operating systems to Windows CE and Palm OS for this release.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.9%, equating to no more than 1 failed access attempt per 1,000 attempts, 24 hours per day, 365 days per year.
generated_requirements.rationale: The 99.9% availability success criterion and technical constraint require continuous system access to support field agents at all hours.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 1,000 simultaneous authenticated users at launch without degradation of performance SLAs.
generated_requirements.rationale: The scalability success criterion requires the system to handle the current agent population of 1,000 concurrent users from day one.

generated_requirements.text: The system architecture shall be designed to scale to support 5,000 simultaneous users within 12 months of launch without requiring a system redesign.
generated_requirements.rationale: The scalability success criterion and technical constraint require a growth-ready architecture to accommodate the projected fivefold increase in realtors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce authentication and authorization on every access attempt, with zero unauthorized access incidents permitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: The security success criterion requires zero unauthorized access incidents, necessitating enforcement on every single access attempt.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all data transmitted between the mobile application and backend systems (office synchronization server, MLS API, authentication server) using industry-standard encryption protocols.
generated_requirements.rationale: Protecting proprietary MLS and client data in transit is essential given the mobile, field-based operating environment and regulatory MLS access rules.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt locally cached data stored on the smart device to prevent unauthorized access if the device is lost or stolen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Offline cached data containing MLS listings and client contacts must be protected at rest on field devices that are at elevated risk of loss.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall store user preferences and cached data locally on the smart device in a manner that survives application restarts and device power cycles.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 requires 100% preference restoration after any failure, which depends on durable local storage that persists across restarts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect synchronization failures (e.g., office system unavailability) and retry the synchronization at the next scheduled interval, logging the failure for IT review.
generated_requirements.rationale: D-01 identifies office system unavailability as a dependency risk; automatic retry and logging ensure resilience and operational visibility.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a clear, user-friendly notification to the realtor when the device transitions to offline mode, indicating which features are unavailable and that previously cached data is accessible.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transparent offline state communication is essential for usability so realtors understand system limitations without confusion or frustration.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall make previously saved search results accessible in offline mode from local device storage.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 explicitly requires that saved search results remain accessible in offline mode to support uninterrupted field productivity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall make previously generated CMA reports accessible in offline mode from local device storage.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 requires offline read access to previously downloaded CMA reports so agents can advise clients even without connectivity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an in-application installation guide that walks an untrained realtor through the setup process step by step, using plain language and real estate domain terminology.
generated_requirements.rationale: The self-service installation constraint requires embedded guidance to achieve the 90% unaided installation success rate without printed materials.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall use a modular, layered software architecture that allows individual components (authentication, sync, MLS integration, mapping) to be updated or replaced independently without impacting other modules.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supporting scale-out to 5,000 users and accommodating future changes to external APIs (MLS, mapping) requires a maintainable, loosely coupled architecture.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with applicable telecommunications and consumer contact regulations when initiating calls to sellers or buyers for appointment scheduling purposes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 7.4 identifies regulatory compliance for consumer communications as a binding constraint on the appointment scheduling feature.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ensure that access to MLS data complies with real estate industry regulations and MLS membership rules, permitting only authorized, registered realtors to query or modify MLS records.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 7.4 identifies MLS access as governed by industry regulations, requiring the system to enforce membership-based authorization on all MLS operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present the realtor with a conflict resolution prompt when a queued offline write operation conflicts with a change made to the same record during the offline period, allowing the realtor to choose which version to retain.
generated_requirements.rationale: The elicitation strategy highlights offline-to-online conflict resolution as a high-risk area requiring explicit handling to maintain data integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete the full hourly synchronization cycle (contacts and appointments) within a time window that does not degrade the responsiveness of other active application features.
generated_requirements.rationale: Background synchronization must not block or slow foreground user interactions, ensuring the 30-second search and 60-second CMA SLAs are maintained during sync.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide the realtor with a visible indicator of the last successful synchronization timestamp so they can assess the currency of their locally cached data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transparency about data freshness supports informed decision-making in the field, particularly when operating near the 60-minute staleness boundary defined in BR-03.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The look and feel of the system shall conform to the user interface standards of the smart device.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The user interface shall have standard menus buttons for navigation
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall have a professional appearance
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall have a consistent color scheme and fonts.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall be used by realtors with no training.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall be intuitive and self explanatory.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be easy for a realtor to learn.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall use symbols and words that are naturally understandable by the realtor community.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall produce search results in an acceptable time
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The search results shall be returned no later 30 seconds after the user has entered the search criteria
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall generate a CMA report in an acceptable time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The CMA report shall be returned no later 60 seconds after the user has entered the CMA report criteria.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall synchronize contacts and appointments in an acceptable time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall synchronize with the office system every hour.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be available for use 24 hours per day 365 days per year
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Out of 1000 accesses to the system the system is available 999 times.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall operate in offline mode whenever internet connection is unavailable.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall allow the user to view previously downloaded search results CMA reports and appointments.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall retain user preferences in the event of a failure.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 100% of saved user preferences shall be restored when system comes back online.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be able to support multiple remote users
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be able support 1000 simultaneous users.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be capable of handling the existing 1000 users. This number is expected to grow 5 times within the next year.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be installed by an untrained realtor without recourse to separately-printed instructions.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 90% of untrained realtors shall be able to install the product on their device without printed instructions
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product is expected to run on Windows CE and Palm operating systems.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Only registered realtors shall be able to access the system.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Every user of the system shall be authenticated and authorized.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall prevent its data from incorrect data being introduced.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall allow a real estate agent to query MLS information
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The user shall be able to get driving directions to a selected property
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The user shall be able to download appointments and contact information for clients
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display clear property images in the search results
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall update or create new property listings in the MLS
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Realtor shall be able to generate a CMA report based on property criteria
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall be able to call the seller or buyer to schedule an appointment
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The realtor shall be notified of new client appointments after automatic synchronization with office system
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The realtor shall be able to retrieve a map showing property locations for a specified area
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall notify the realtor when a seller or buyer responds to an appointment request
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall allow the user to save the property search results.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR
